oneLiner: A light, refreshing protein drink that's the easy, feel-good upgrade for wellness-minded lifestyles.
positioningStatement: PEP is a premium protein drink that supports healthier, happier routines – not with gym-bro energy, but as a delicious, light treat you genuinely want. Designed for brunches, beach days, and coworking breaks, PEP fits seamlessly into modern, style-conscious lives, bringing wellness without the sacrifice.
audience: Health-conscious adults seeking tasty, functional drinks beyond basic hydration
Urban professionals and creatives who care about self-image and daily wellbeing
People who want healthy habits but reject extreme routines or restrictive diets
Social and wellness-oriented consumers: brunch-goers, café lovers, coworking regulars
occasions: Post-yoga or Pilates treat (not heavy post-gym)
Any time a refreshing, uplifting boost fits into a balanced lifestyle

visual.mood: Pastel, fresh, minimal, light, with accents of playful warmth (orangeLight, peach, cream, green).
visual.photography: Lifestyle-driven: bright, natural moments at cafes, sunny outdoor gatherings, desk-side refreshment. Muted backgrounds, light-filled, effortless enjoyment.
visual.typography: Premium, modern elegance: Didot wordmark, Montserrat for numerals/body, soft script for tagline; avoid aggressive/techy/geometric fonts.
rejectLooks: overly clinical or functional label design
masculine, dark, or neon color palettes

inLineBrands.name: BLOOM
inLineBrands.why: Integrates prebiotic benefits with fun, lively lifestyle branding. Wellness focused without being medicinal or gym-like.

inLineBrands.name: EVER TREE
inLineBrands.why: Minimalist, elegant natural drinks for healthier everyday choices. Focus on simplicity and subtle design.

inLineBrands.name: OLIPOP
inLineBrands.why: Soft, health-focused branding aimed at modern wellness. Accessible but stylish, not hardcore or medicinal.

inLineBrands.name: POPP
inLineBrands.why: Health-centric, vibrant, and refreshing design with broad lifestyle appeal.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Primarily a functional, vitamin-enriched drink with a clinical, health-first approach – lacks lifestyle and emotional connection. Not about identity or enjoyment.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Targeted at functional hydration and performance, more clinical and minimalist with a utility-first message. Lacks lifestyle or enjoyment cues.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Sports/gym-centric BCAA/energy drink brand, bold and performance-oriented; not suitable for leisure, social or wellness lifestyle context.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Experimental protein/alcohol hybrid – leans social but in a niche way, not focused on wellness or everyday lifestyle moments.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Mainly positioned as a hydration and functional wellness drink, less about lightness or treat-like enjoyment, with more clinical branding.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Kin Euphorics
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Highly social and mood-enhancing, designed around night-out or festival energy. Focuses on replacement for alcohol, not light/lifestyle wellness.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Lyre's
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Premium non-alcoholic spirits for social occasions – too 'occasion-based', rooted in social ritual vs. everyday wellness moments.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Seedlip
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: High-end, social, non-alcoholic spirits. Beautiful, but more event/festival/late evening than daily lifestyle wellness.

antiReferences.name: Lean Body / gym RTD protein drinks
antiReferences.why: Aggressively gym/masculine, functional/clinical; doesn't fit lifestyle wellness or subtle enjoyment. Rigid performance tone.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: US probiotic/energy drink with a similar name but unrelated positioning, function, and branding. To avoid confusion and misalignment.

antiReferences.name: Any 'energy drink' aesthetic
antiReferences.why: Harsh colors, bold type, or 'boost' messaging is not welcome – it contradicts lightness, wellness, and enjoyment.

layoutOnlyReferences.name: Revized
patterns: simple navigation / focus on user-friendly experience

brandFitRules: No gym or sports drink aesthetics (bold, aggressive, high-contrast, chrome/neon).
No performance, muscle-growth, or intense function-first language.
Copy and visuals must feel bright, natural, and inviting (never clinical or medical).
Packaging and digital experience leverage pastel, minimal backgrounds with playful color accents.
Typography: Premium, modern serif for logo, friendly sans-serif for info, script only for soft touches and tagline.
Photography: Always lifestyle-driven, soft-focused, never harsh, staged, or crowded.
Avoid festival/party maximalism and event-centric branding.
References limited to stylish wellness and subtle lifestyle beverage peers (BLOOM, OLIPOP, POPP, Ever Tree).
No allusions to drinkpep.com in naming, function, or branding.
Always emphasize enjoyment and fit with modern, wellness-minded lifestyles.
